channel.title: Help.medium.com on Chege's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Help.medium.com on Chege's Blog

item.title: 02. The Subject
item.description:
The Subject

The number of words aren’t intuitive to me. Adults tend to read non-fiction at 238 words per minute. So a good test for how appropriate a piece of writing is, “Can the writer cover X in N minutes?”

Hugo uses 213 WPM, while Medium uses 265 WPM, suggesting Medium has a bias for skimming over close reading.

I wonder if a browser extension that rates the appropriateness of articles given their subject and their word count is a feasible/desirable thing. Then again, we have Twitter with 140 characters ლ(ಠ_ಠ ლ)
